title: Vinesh Phogat knocked out of Asian Games trials after semifinal loss to Meenakshi
content: The defeat ended Vinesh's campaign after an eventful day in which she had first secured permission to compete in the 53kg category and then battled her way into the last four.

title: Karnataka CLP elects DK Shivakumar as leader; CM oath ceremony on June 3
content: DK Shivakumar has been elected as the new leader of the Congress Legislature Party in Bengaluru. This decision follows Siddaramaiah's resignation as Karnataka chief minister. Shivakumar's astrologer has recommended June 3rd at 5:15 PM for his oath-taking ceremony at Lok Bhavan.

title: Operation Sindoor 2.0: How the army is preparing for the next battle
content: India's Army is undergoing a significant transformation post-Operation Sindoor, shifting towards preemptive, technology-driven deterrence. New formations like Rudra Brigades and Bhairav battalions, alongside integrated drone units, are enhancing combat readiness. This modernization focuses on unmanned systems, AI, and rapid response, reflecting a decisive move from manpower-heavy tactics to a high-tech, multi-domain force.

title: Rain lashes parts of Delhi-NCR; IMD issues yellow alert
content: Delhi-NCR experienced a welcome respite from the heat as heavy rains brought down temperatures significantly on Saturday. The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for light rain, thunderstorms, and gusty winds, with further temperature drops anticipated.  Air quality has also seen improvement, moving into the satisfactory category.

title: Google CEO called out 'biggest AI budget problem' of companies world over from IO stage with a solution
content: Google's CEO Sundar Pichai highlighted the escalating costs of AI, with companies depleting annual budgets by May due to heavy token usage. To combat this, Google unveiled Gemini 3.5 Flash, a cost-effective model promising significant savings. This new AI aims to boost efficiency in coding and agentic tasks, potentially saving enterprises over a billion dollars annually.

title: Artistry vs Adrenaline: Gill owns the night, Sooryavanshi wins hearts
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless 96 once again showcased the teenager's extraordinary talent, but it was Shubman Gill's sublime century that carried Gujarat Titans into the IPL final. While Sooryavanshi provided the thrills, Gill's masterclass in timing and placement sealed a record playoff chase and a date with RCB.

title: Truck carrying Afghan refugees from Pakistan overturns, killing 22
content: A tragic road accident in eastern Afghanistan claimed at least 22 lives, including 10 children and five women, as a truck carrying returning refugees overturned. Officials reported around 36 others injured. The incident, attributed to the driver reportedly falling asleep, highlights the perilous journeys of Afghans returning from Pakistan and Iran amidst increased expulsions.

title: CUET chaos: Uncertainty & tears as students left in lurch at Noida centre
content: Students faced significant disruptions during the CUET-UG 2026 examination due to technical glitches and administrative confusion. Many were left waiting for hours at centers, with some exams not starting at all, leading to protests and questions about the NTA's operational preparedness.

title: 'Amazon would collapse if run like New York City': Jeff Bezos takes swipe Mamdani
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has endorsed New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new government efficiency initiative, COGE. This comes after their public disagreement on taxing the wealthy. Bezos, who previously criticized city spending, now supports Mamdani's plan to make government work smarter, suggesting savings could benefit lower earners.

title: US cop fines woman for texting while driving - why it caused big row
content: A Florida woman, ticketed for holding a cellphone in her right hand while driving despite lacking one, had her citation dismissed. The Palm Beach County Sheriff's Office requested the dismissal due to insufficient evidence after the unusual traffic stop gained viral attention. The deputy's initial observation was contradicted by video evidence, leading to the case's resolution.

title: Train staff caught washing utensils in toilet; IRCTC gets notice - video
content: FSSAI has issued a statutory notice to IRCTC after a viral video showed catering staff washing utensils in a train toilet on the Lokmanya Tilak Terminus-Ernakulam Duronto Express. The food regulator called the act extremely unhygienic and a serious food safety concern, demanding an immediate action taken report and explanation.

title: 2 wives, domestic abuse, deadly plot: Chilling details in ex-Congress sarpanch's family murder
content: A shocking quadruple murder has rocked Ajmer, with police confirming the deaths of a former sarpanch and three family members were not accidental but a planned act. Investigators allege the man's first wife and their two children are responsible, stemming from alleged domestic abuse following his second marriage. The victims' charred bodies were found in a burnt SUV.

title: 'AI is rapidly commoditising': Zoho founder quotes Oracle's Larry Ellison on future of AI
content: Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu echoed Larry Ellison's view that AI is rapidly commoditizing, with value shifting to applications built around it. Ellison argued that similar public data training makes AI models less of a differentiator, emphasizing exclusive datasets as the future competitive edge. Vembu also recently described the AI boom as a significant investment bubble.

title: 'Even if you slit my throat ... ': TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee defiant after FIR, CID summons
content: Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee has defiantly responded to summons from the West Bengal CID, vowing not to be intimidated by investigative agencies.  He stated that even facing severe threats would not make him yield.  The summons follows a series of legal challenges and a recent visit by Kolkata Police to his residence, which was described as routine.

title: ‘Nobody wants to see another Ram’: Arun Govil speaks on backlash over Ranbir Kapoor's ‘Ramayana’
content: Veteran actor Arun Govil, who will play King Dasharath in Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana, said nobody wants to see another Rama, explaining the backlash Ranbir Kapoor faced. However, he called Ranbir a good actor. Ranbir also revealed he will play both Lord Rama and Lord Parshurama in the film releasing on Diwali 2026.

title: 80 launch pads, 3 octagon installations: 'Alarming' Chinese buildup near nuke silos
content: According to images examined by Reuters, China has built more than 80 launch pads along with three large octagon-shaped facilities near the Hami nuclear missile silo field in the country's north-west. The launch pads could be used by mobile missile launchers and air-defence systems, while the larger facilities may support communications, electronic warfare and military command functions, according to security analysts who reviewed the imagery.

title: A year after India broke Pak's offensive during Op Sindoor, Joint Air Defence Doctrine release
content: India's defence forces are enhancing aerial protection with the new Joint Air Defence Doctrine, integrating Army, Navy, and Air Force for a unified shield. This move, following successful operations and global vulnerabilities, aims to counter modern threats like drones and hypersonic weapons.  Technological advancements, including new missile systems, bolster this multi-layered defence strategy.

title: No need to leave US for green card, DHS clarifies
content: US Homeland Security has clarified that green card applicants generally don't need to leave the country during processing, easing confusion from an earlier announcement. While officers retain case-by-case discretion, especially for those with visa overstays or reliance on public aid, no major policy shift has occurred.

title: 'NEET, CBSE, SSC. And today CUET': Rahul says 'not a single exam conducted with honesty'
content: Rahul Gandhi and opposition leaders have strongly criticized the Centre following widespread technical glitches that delayed the CUET-UG 2026 exam. This incident, following similar issues with NEET, CBSE, and SSC, has fueled accusations of systemic failures in conducting national examinations fairly and efficiently, raising concerns about student futures.

title: TOSS SSC, Inter result 2026 out: Direct link to download marks memo here
content: The Telangana Open School Society (TOSS) has officially announced the SSC (Class 10) and Intermediate (Class 12) Public examination results for 2026 on its website, telanganaopenschool.org. Students who appeared for these flexible learning examinations can now access and download their marks memos by entering their hall ticket details. Candidates can check the direct link to download hall tickets here.

title: India confined Pakistan navy to its ports during Operation Sindoor: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence minister Rajnath Singh revealed the Indian Navy's decisive role in Operation Sindoor, keeping Pakistan's navy confined to ports. Speaking at the Lucknow naval museum inauguration, he highlighted the operation's success following the Pahalgam terror attack. Singh also lauded India's growing defence manufacturing and export capabilities.

title: 70% drop in gold demand: Import duty hike from 6% to 15% rattles buyers
content: India's gold demand has plummeted by nearly 70% following a government increase in import duty from 6% to 15%. This, coupled with rising fuel and food prices, has significantly dampened consumer sentiment and spending on discretionary items like gold.

title: 'Not going to vacate': Rabri's 'evict by force' dare to CM Samrat over bungalow
content: The RJD leader reacted sharply when reporters questioned her about a recent order issued by the state's building construction department allotting the 10, Circular Road residence in Patna to Dairy and Fisheries Minister Nand Kishor Ram. "Yes, I can see that Samrat Choudhary, who has become the chief minister only recently, is quite excited. Let him get me evicted by force. I am not going to vacate the premises", said the indignant wife of RJD president Lalu Prasad.

title: India shows ‘cautious resilience’ despite Middle East conflict: DEA
content: India's economy shows 'cautious resilience' despite global headwinds like Middle East conflict and rising oil prices. While manufacturing and services remain strong, and forex reserves offer a buffer, inflation concerns are mounting due to wholesale price surges. A weaker monsoon could further challenge growth and rural demand.

title: Gujarat man sentenced to 10 years in prison without parole in minor trafficking case
content: A Nebraska hotel theft complaint unraveled a child trafficking ring involving Gujarati-origin operators. Kavankumar Patel received a 10-year sentence for sex trafficking minors. Investigations revealed hidden cameras and exploitation of victims, with Falguni Choudhary allegedly watching forced sex acts. Authorities continue to pursue traffickers.

title: How English-to-Hindi shift in many govt schools has Delhi parents, kids in a fix
content: Students in Delhi government schools are struggling as English-medium classes in Class XI are being discontinued. This forces them to choose between Hindi-medium or changing schools. Parents and teachers report negative impacts on academic performance and confidence. Education activists are calling for an inquiry into the matter to protect students' futures and ensure equal opportunities.

title: Why 8 in 10 patients turn to internet after doctor visits
content: A recent survey reveals a significant "patient confusion crisis" in Delhi-NCR, with nearly 80% of patients seeking online clarification post-doctor visits. Many feel rushed, lack guidance on next steps, and struggle to navigate hospitals, leading to increased costs and stress. Experts highlight overcrowded OPDs and weak guidance systems as key issues.

title: Is Indus Treaty deadlock hurting Pak? Karachi reels under chronic water shortage
content: Karachi faces a severe water crisis during peak summer, coinciding with Eid-ul-Adha. Jamaat-e-Islami Pakistan chief Hafiz Naeem ur Rehman has accused the ruling PPP of gross mismanagement and failure to address the city's chronic water shortages despite 18 years in power.  Nearly 70% of the city is reportedly experiencing extended supply disruptions, forcing residents to buy expensive private tankers.

title: Terror plot foiled: Nine nabbed for planning Delhi attack with ISI, underworld help
content: Delhi Police's Special Cell arrested nine terrorists linked to Pakistan's ISI and the Mumbai underworld, who were planning attacks on vital installations and security personnel in the capital. This operation follows a significant overhaul of Delhi Police's counter-terrorism framework, including the establishment of dedicated Counter-Terror Units in every police station to enhance intelligence gathering and threat identification.

title: Tesla slashes Model Y price by up to Rs 9 lakh in India to spur demand
content: Tesla has significantly reduced Model Y prices in India, with the Premium RWD now costing Rs 9 lakh less. This strategic move aims to boost demand for its imported EVs, making them more accessible to a wider audience.

title: Big B Hails Teen Cricketer: Amitabh Bachchan praises Vaibhav Sooryavanshi; fans celebrate his 96
content: Amitabh Bachchan praised 15-year-old cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after Rajasthan Royals' IPL exit, saying he couldn't even play gulli danda at that age. Vaibhav scored 96 runs off 47 balls but was heartbroken after the loss. Fans across social media showered him with immense love and support.

title: Matt Walsh Slams Erika Kirk Boyfriend Rumors as Blake Wynn Breaks Silence
content: Matt Walsh criticized an X user for spreading claims that Erika Kirk was dating Blake Wynn. The rumor originated from Project Constitution, which alleged the two were seen together at a Beverly Hills hotel. Erika Kirk strongly denied the accusation, saying she was in Arizona with her family. Blake Wynn also rejected the rumor and said they are not dating.

title: Inside Rod Brind’Amour and Amy Biedenbach’s Enduring Love Story Beyond the NHL
content: As Rod Brind’Amour guides the Carolina Hurricanes through another strong NHL season. And fans are curious about the woman beside him, Amy Biedenbach, his wife since 2010, who plays a powerful yet quiet role in his journey; their bond reflects not only enduring love but shared values and community-driven goals that keep them deeply connected.

title: 'India preparing for Operation Sindoor 2.0': Army chief Upendra Dwivedi
content: India is preparing for 'Operation Sindoor 2.0' as Army chief Upendra Dwivedi stated the military posture in response to Pakistan's actions remains in place. The operation, a continuation of last year's retaliatory response to terrorism, involves enhancing synergy among all three services for future warfare. The focus is on cautious deployment and troop protection in border areas.

title: 'It'll be suicidal': Robin on tactical battle that could decide PSG vs Arsenal in CL Final
content: PSG carry the weight of champions, refusing to stop as they hope to retain the crown. Arsenal arrive with carpe diem in their hearts and history at their backs as they find themselves in the final of the Champions League for the first time since 2006.  And if Arsenal get over the line, they would also become only the 25th club to ever win the European Cup or Champions League, having played 225 games in the competition - more than any other club to have never lifted the trophy.

title: 'PM Modi supervised NEET paper leak': Rahul's big charge as Centre gears up for retest
content: Rahul Gandhi sharply criticized Prime Minister Modi over the NEET UG paper leak, accusing him of personally supervising the exam's integrity. This follows the Centre's assurance to the Supreme Court about the upcoming re-examination. The apex court expressed concern for students, while the NTA detailed extensive security reforms and new safeguards implemented to prevent future irregularities.

title: 'It hurts deeply': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi shares RR's post after IPL 2026 exit
content: Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 campaign ended in heartbreak after a Qualifier 2 loss to Gujarat Titans. Despite a brilliant 96 from 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the team fell short. Sooryavanshi's stellar season, marked by maturity and exceptional batting against top bowlers, has earned him high praise and suggestions of an imminent India call-up.

title: Ten years after India, US Marine Corps bids farewell to Sea Harrier
content: The US Marine Corps is retiring its AV-8B Sea Harrier Jump Jet, a decade after India's navy did. This unique aircraft's Vertical Take-Off and Landing capability allowed operations from dispersed locations and smaller carriers, proving vital for close air support and deployments. This technology lives on in the F-35B.

title: 'India is powerful and modernising its military': US defence secretary Pete Hegseth
content: US defence secretary Pete Hegseth lauded India's military modernization and strategic importance in the Indo-Pacific. He highlighted New Delhi as a critical anchor for regional balance and praised its growing industrial capacity for high-end military operations.  The US is pursuing co-production initiatives with India, including for Javelin munitions, to enhance collective readiness.

title: Need low-cost housing? Here's how the SPARSH 2.0 scheme works
content: The Union Territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu has launched SPARSH 2.0, a new housing initiative to boost affordable rental homes for workers and the economically weaker sections. Government agencies and PSUs are encouraged to build or convert complexes into low-cost rental units, aiming to enhance living conditions and support the 'Housing for All' mission.

title: No one-man show: How RCB became a complete unit
content: RCB's Director of Cricket Mo Bobat believes the franchise's new identity matches with Rajat Patidar's. Built on fearless intent and collective contributions, RCB's second consecutive title push is strengthened by the experience of Virat Kohli, Krunal Pandya, Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood. They will face Gujarat Titans in the final on Sunday.

title: Trump's medical report: US president 'fully fit' to work but asked to shed weight
content: President Trump's latest medical report declared him in 'excellent health' and fit to serve, despite advising weight loss and increased physical activity. The report noted his weight has increased, and he takes medication for cholesterol and cardiac prevention. Bruising on his hands was attributed to frequent handshaking while on aspirin.

title: Internet services to be suspended on May 30 in Haryana’s Faridabad ahead of demolition drive
content: 2G, 3G, 4G and 5G, bulk SMS services, and dongle services will be suspended within a one-kilometre radius of the notified location in NIT Zone, Faridabad district, and the suspension will remain in force from 12:30 a.m. to 10 p.m. on May 30

title: Proposed Kerala high-speed rail to connect Thiruvananthapuram with Kannur in 3.5 hours
content: The new interim report on the high-speed rail project, submitted by technocrat E. Sreedharan to the State government, envisages it as the world’s first green rail corridor

title: Undertrial prisoners entitled to be treated at private hospitals of their choice: Madras High Court
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan permit Devanathan Yadav of Mylapore Hindu Permanent Fund Nidhi Limited to undergo treatment at a private hospital for 10 weeks

title: Discussions held on housing, pension, drinking water issues during DISHA meeting in Visakhapatnam
content: TDP’s Palla Srinivasa Rao noted that despite allocations having been made nearly two decades ago in some cases, a significant number of beneficiaries had yet to take possession or begin residing in the units

title: Six persons injured in armed attack in Tenkasi district
content: Without any apparent provocation, the assailants drew sickles and attacked six persons, five of whom were members of SC communities

title: Navy Chief underlines U.P.’s contributions to force at ‘Shaurya Vatika’ opening
content: Navy Chief Admiral Tripathi praises Uttar Pradesh’s vital contributions to the Indian Navy at the Nausena Shaurya Vatika inauguration

title: Three women devotees, four children faint in Thirupparankundram temple queue as Vaikasi Visakam draws large crowd
content: With the temperature hovering around 34 degrees Celsius, some women carrying infants expressed uneasiness and discomfort, having arrived at the temple as early as 6 a.m.

title: Lokesh established himself as a supporter of women’s cause, says MP Shabari
content: Shabari said while previous Mahanadu’s saw the participation of around five lakh cadres, this year’s event organized in a digital-hybrid format across various clusters set a new world record, with participation of 17 lakh TDP cadre

title: ‘Remove obstructions for free flow of water in Amaravati’: ADC
content: The Amaravati Development Corporation chairperson issued instructions to engineering officials after reviewing the situation at the pumping station

title: Andhra Pradesh government permits ASI survey in Nallamala forests
content: Mr. Kalyan stated that the conservation of forests was important not only from the biodiversity point of view but also bringing to light historical, archaeological, and cultural treasures that might be hidden somewhere deep in the woods

title: Police arrests former MLA Balka Suman from Telangana Bhavan
content: The former MLA had been staying at Telangana Bhavan since police registered a case against him on May 27 following his remarks at a meeting

title: Congress govt. in Himachal changing election rules to hijack local body polls: BJP State chief
content: , which should have been conducted in October-November 2025, were "deliberately delayed, pushing the State towards a constitutional crisis", Rajeev Bindal said

title: Education system has fallen into hands of mafia: Arvind Kejriwal
content: Reacting to reports that NEET papers would be transported to exam centres by the Indian Air Force, Mr. Kejriwal said that Centre has no intention of getting to the bottom of the paper leaks, and was instead making such bizarre moves

title: Indian Navy confined entire Pakistan Navy to its ports during Operation Sindoor: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh
content: Indian Navy was deployed in the Arabian Sea with full preparedness and strength. As a result, Pakistan's entire Navy remained confined to its ports, says Defence Minister Rajnath Singh

title: Over 45k booth-level officers engaged to carry out SIR in Odisha from May 30
content: "If anyone is found to be an outsider or foreign national, his/her name will be omitted from the electoral roll," the CEO said

title: Twisha Sharma dowry-harassment case: A timeline of events
content: The high-profile dowry-harassment case involving 33-year old Twisha Sharma in Bhopal has developed into a story of alleged institutional bias, procedural lapses and evidence tampering.

title: Tirthahalli co-operative society president claims inquiry based on false complaints by loan defaulters
content: The Department of Co-operation recently ordered inquiries into three societies following directions from the Chief Minister’s office, based on complaints filed by a few shareholders

title: D.K. Shivakumar to be sworn-in as Karnataka chief minister at Glass House in Lok Bhavan on June 3
content: KPCC Working President G.C. Chandrashekar appeals to party workers not to come to venue to reduce inconvenience to people

title: Muslim group in Bihar raises demand on cow protection and ban on beef export
content: Members of the Muslim community in Bihar’s Darbhanga raised demands to declare the cow as the national animal and ban beef exports during a peaceful gathering after Eid-ul-Adha prayers. The event highlighted calls for respect, harmony, and social unity.

title: AHINDA leaders seek Deputy CM post for Yathindra, voice concern over Siddaramaiah’s exit
content: They cautioned that the political development could affect the Congress party’s prospects unless the new leadership takes AHINDA communities into confidence, and ensures equal representation in the government

title: Thunderstorms, rain likely to continue in Rajasthan for next 4-5 days
content: In the past 24 hours, several parts of the State witnessed strong thunderstorms and rainfall, with rain in Dholpur district receiving the highest rainfall of 58 mm

title: Claims of being vishwaguru but can't conduct single exam: Rahul's dig at PM
content: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi’s remarks came after the National Testing Agency said the CUET-UG 2026 for admissions into undergraduate degree programmes across India was delayed at some centres on Saturday due to a technical glitch

title: Defence secretary meets officials from Australia, EU, Netherlands in Singapore
content: The India-Australia defence relationship has acquired greater strategic significance in recent years amid growing focus on security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region

title: K. Madhu quits as Kerala State Film Development Corporation chairperson
content: He was continuing in the position for some time even after previous LDF government directed all political appointees to vacate their posts following change of guard in State administration

title: Evict me by force, says Rabri Devi on her bungalow being allotted to a Bihar Minister
content: ‘Yes, I can see that Samrat Choudhary, who has become the Chief Minister only recently, is quite excited. Let him get me evicted by force. I am not going to vacate the premises,’ said Rabri

title: U.S. War Secretary Hegseth backs Trump’s India-Pak ceasefire claim; outlines Indo-Pacific strategy
content: The U.S. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said both India and Pakistan would continue to view each other through the prism of security concerns

title: Mandya gears up for new academic year; ZP CEO reviews school readiness
content: She reviewed preparations related to school cleanliness, building safety, maintenance of records, teacher responsibilities, student enrolment drives, distribution of textbooks and uniforms, mainstreaming of out-of-school children, and readiness of the Akshara Dasoha (mid-day meal) programme

title: Himachal panchayat elections: Rain fails to dampen voter enthusiasm, women outpoll men
content: The overall voter turnout was 62.6% at 1 p.m.; Sirmaur district had the highest voter turnout at 71.28%, while Lahaul and Spiti registered the lowest at 58.61%

title: Donald Trump's daughter visits Taj Mahal
content: Tiffany Trump visits the Taj Mahal, exploring its history and beauty with her husband, amid high-security arrangements in Agra

title: Properties worth ₹2 crore linked to drug trafficking attached in J&K
content: Srinagar police attached a residential house and land worth approximately ₹50 lakh belonging to alleged drug peddler Shafiq Ahmad Khawaja, a resident of Dildar Karnah, in north Kashmir's Kupwara district

title: D.K. Shivakumar: Congress troubleshooter poised to become Chief Minister of Karnataka
content: D.K. Shivakumar’s proximity to the party’s top brass, particularly Sonia Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, is believed to have strengthened his claim after a prolonged power struggle with Chief Minister Siddaramaiah over the last three years

title: Armed forces ready for ‘Operation Sindoor 2.0’ if need arises, says Army chief
content: General Dwivedi noted that while a temporary cessation of hostilities currently exists, all three services are preparing intensely for the next phase should it take place

title: West Bengal set to implement National Health Mission, says CM Suvendu Adhikari
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said the Centre has sanctioned ₹2,103 crore for the scheme, out of which we have already received ₹527 crore

title: Six killed, 40 injured as tractor-trolley overturns in M.P.’s Anuppur
content: Around 50 people were travelling in the tractor-trolley when it overturned after the driver lost control, Additional Superintendent of Police Jagannath Markam said

title: T.N. Minister Sengottaiyan dismisses allegations of horse-trading by TVK
content: Referring to allegations levelled by Opposition parties, Mr. Sengottaiyan said the government would respond to the issue of ‘horse-trading’ on the floor of the Assembly

title: ‘Technical glitch’ delays CUET-UG 2026 exam at some centres
content: Noting that the issue has since been resolved, NTA said that the “exam is being conducted with full compensatory time so that no candidate is disadvantaged”

title: CBSE OSM row revives questions over firm linked to Telangana’s 2019 Intermediate results controversy
content: Leader of the Opposition, Rahul Gandhi, posted on social media recalling how Coempt Edu Tech was the new name of Globarena Technologies involved in the Intermediate marks fiasco in Telangana

title: Manoj Jarange Patil launches ninth indefinite fast over Maratha quota demands
content: The activist began the fast in Antarwali Sarati village, about 400 km from Mumbai, in the open without any canopy or protective shelter despite the intense heat

title: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari launches cervical cancer vaccination drive for over 7.5 lakh girls aged 14 years
content: Under the campaign, girls who have completed 14 years of age but are yet to turn 15 will receive a single dose of the quadrivalent Gardasil-4 vaccine free of cost

title: Pune hooch tragedy: 22 police, Excise personnel face suspension
content: Eight persons, including a key bootlegger, have been arrested in connection with the tragedy that unfolded across Pimpri-Chinchwad and Pune city over a span of two days

title: Defence land in Ammuguda encroached, old rock formations destroyed
content: Ammuguda hillocks are home to ancient rock formations and open scrub lands which are used by the society members and local residents for trekking, and rock climbing activities

title: Will D.K. Shivakumar’s Cabinet strike the right balance? | Decode Karnataka
content: What does the change of guard mean for the Congress in Karnataka? What are the key challenges staring at CM to-be D.K. Shivakumar?

title: Myanmar President Aung Hlaing begins India visit
content: Mr. Aung Hlaing kicked off his trip by landing in Gaya to visit the Mahabodhi temple, ahead of his arrival in Delhi

title: Pattabiram residents demand limited-use subway beneath ROB
content: Though one carriageway of the six-lane Rail Over Bridge (ROB) was opened to traffic in September 2024, no provision has been made for slow-moving traffic that also need to cross Level Crossing No. 2 (Railway Siding) at Pattabiram

title: Elephant calf rescued after falling into water tank near Anthiyur in T.N.
content: Rescue personnel first pumped out the water from the tank using an electric motor. A JCB excavator was then pressed into service to breach a portion of the structure and create a passage for the animal to climb out

title: Heavy rain and thunderstorms batter Kolkata and surrounding
content: Air, rail, and metro services hit as a squall passes over Kolkata; several cars across Kolkata and Salt Lake were crushed under uprooted trees

title: 22 Dead As Truck Carrying Refugees From Pakistan Overturns In Afghanistan
content: Among the dead were 10 children and five women, and the injured were taken to hospitals in Nangarhar.

title: Journalist Assaulted, Media Office Vandalised In Assam's Silchar
content: The management of Barak Bani claimed that the journalist had previously faced intimidation over reports concerning alleged illegal activities, including betting and gambling operations.

title: Netanyahu Worried Trump May Strike A "Bad Interim Deal" With Iran: Report
content: For Israel, concerns remain that any emerging deal may leave out key issues such as Iran’s enriched uranium stockpile, its missile programme and support for regional proxy groups.

title: Vajiram And Ravi IAS Study Centre Fined Rs 7 Lakh For 'Misleading Ads'
content: The decision, said the CCPA, was taken to protect and promote the rights of consumers as a class and to ensure that no false or misleading advertisement is made

title: UP Residents To Pay 10% Fuel Surcharge On June Electricity Bills
content: The surcharge has been imposed to recover the additional expenditure incurred on power procurement and transmission costs in accordance with regulations framed by the Uttar Pradesh Electricity Regulatory Commission.

title: 9 With Links To Pak's ISI, Dawood Ibrahim Arrested, Were Planning Attacks In Delhi, Mumbai
content: The Delhi Police's Special Cell said the accused are residents of Delhi, Mumbai, and Punjab, and some are foreign nationals.

title: 'Kuki Militants Took Tactical Positions Along Route': Manipur Police On Ambush
content: The Manipur Police said the convoy "came under heavy attack in... a hill area by armed Kuki militants who had taken tactical positions along the route"

title: El Nino Likely To Result In Deficient Indian Monsoon, Raises Crop Concerns
content: Meteorologists are watching the Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD), another major climate phenomenon that occasionally offsets El Nino's drying influence on India.

title: As The War Rages In Iran, Tensions Brew In Other Parts Of The World
content: North Korea's nuclear defiance, a drone strike in Romania, China's silo expansion and Kazakhstan's offer to store Tehran's uranium stockpile present themselves as multiple flashpoints.

title: Arunachal Pradesh To Form Panels To Review ST Certificates, Infiltration
content: The committees have been asked to submit their recommendations within six months. However, the timeline may be extended depending on field studies and stakeholder consultations.

title: US Sanctions Iranian Buyers Of Military Technology
content: The United States announced Friday that it is "dismantling a sophisticated Iranian network" used to...

title: Trivia Champ, Helpful Grandfather Among Washington Tank Collapse Victims
content: The collapse expelled a flood of caustic chemicals powerful enough to blow out building walls and overturn pickup trucks at the site.

title: ICE Allows Deported Mother To Return To US For Her 3-Year-Old Son's Funeral
content: The 3-year-old was killed by his own relative after his mother was deported from the US in January this year.

title: Epstein Aide Ghislaine Maxwell Should "Die In Prison": Ex-US Attorney Told Lawmakers
content: Maxwell was a longtime associate of Epstein, the US financier and convicted sex offender, who died by suicide in jail in 2019.

title: Iran War LIVE Updates: Trump's Meet With Aides Ends Without "Final Determination"

title: Mumbai Man Keeps Wife In 'Rooster' Position For 4 Hours, Stabs As She Escapes
